behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure boundary is parser -> hook-input handling handoff, not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; in-process wiring is sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: a unit test of parser alone could miss escaping/field propagation bugs across modules. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System is disqualified by behavior text 'parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling' (no requirement for real process/OS I/O).
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process test that feeds valid JSON fixtures containing code fences, backticks, and `$vars` through the same parser + input-handling pipeline used by hooks, then assert successful parse and expected downstream handled state.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior depends on interaction between parse failure classification and trace record construction. REAL-INFRA: no real subprocess/OS needed to verify trace payload content. MOCK-HIDE: mocking trace sink too aggressively could hide missing context fields, so use real in-process sink implementation. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System is disqualified because behavior only requires malformed payload -> trace record content, not process lifecycle.
behaviors.test_description: Use malformed JSON fixtures in an in-process pipeline with real parser and real trace formatter/sink adapter, then assert `json_parse_failed` plus diagnostic context (offset/snippet/error message) is present.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is policy mapping across parser error object -> policy handler -> telemetry intent marker, which is a module boundary interaction. REAL-INFRA: local in-process test is enough; no OS/network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: unit-testing only mapper logic could miss actual no-throw behavior when wired to parser error type. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System is disqualified by 'local error-policy path'.
behaviors.test_description: Inject malformed JSON into parser wired to the real local policy handler and telemetry-intent flagging path; assert returned handled result, no exception escape, and telemetry intent set.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly requires real process execution, malformed stdin handling, and exit behavior. MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes can miss stdin buffering/EOF/exit-order issues that appear only in subprocess runtime. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: Integration is disqualified by explicit text 'In real process execution' and 'stdin...before exit'.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the hook executable as a subprocess, pipe malformed stdin, wait for completion, and assert non-crash exit semantics plus emitted trace signal in captured output/log channel before process exit.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: explicit end-to-end invocation path includes real stdin and real hook runtime path. MOCK-HIDE: faking invocation can hide serialization/transport/order issues between process input and trace artifact emission. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: Integration is disqualified by behavior text 'real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink)'.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the actual hook entrypoint with malformed stdin and assert the produced trace artifact from the real sink (or real sink adapter) matches expected `json_parse_failed` semantics.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: distinction requires exercising both payload classes through shared parse+classification+trace pathways, not only parser unit logic. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required if same in-process pipeline is used. MOCK-HIDE: isolated unit parser tests may not catch misattribution introduced in downstream error labeling. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System is disqualified because behavior is classification/regression distinction, not process runtime semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven in-process regression suite: run valid markdown-heavy JSON and truly malformed JSON through the same parser/trace classification pipeline; assert success path for valid cases and `json_parse_failed` attribution only for malformed cases.
